user_query:
I’m preparing a fast, reproducible snapshot of sector-focused investment and trade announcements across key Australian agencies for an investor brief. Using the provided inputs, please implement and run a small script-based workflow that downloads, extracts, classifies, and summarizes content from the agencies’ own websites.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/sources.csv: list of official agency domains and regions to target.
- input/sectors.csv: mapping of sector names to classification keywords (semicolon-separated).

Requirements:
1) Discovery and download (Internet required): For each row in input/sources.csv, treat the domain as the root (no hardcoded URLs). Programmatically discover one internal “news-style” landing page by fetching the home page and scanning internal links whose paths or link text include any of: news, media, press, stories, insights, or blog. Download that landing page and then up to 10 article pages linked from it that:
   - stay within the same domain;
   - are HTML (skip PDFs and other binaries);
   - contain in their URL path or visible link text at least one of: invest, trade, export, industry, or any keyword from input/sectors.csv.
   Save all fetched HTML under data/raw/<slug>/ where <slug> is a filesystem-safe version of the source name (e.g., austrade -> austrade). Filenames must be deterministic (e.g., a hash of the URL or a sanitized slug). Also write a CSV manifest at data/fetch_manifest.csv with columns: source,domain,region,url,type (landing|article),http_status,local_html_path,bytes.

2) Extraction and classification: From each saved article page, extract a structured record and write one JSON object per line to data/extracted/items.jsonl with fields:
   - source (string)
   - domain (string)
   - region (string)
   - url (string)
   - local_html_path (string; must match a real file under data/raw)
   - title (string; use <title> or first h1/h2 as fallback)
   - publish_date (ISO-8601 date if present, else null; try common meta tags or date patterns)
   - text_excerpt (first ~200 characters of visible text)
   - word_count (int; words in the visible text)
   - sectors (array of sector names from input/sectors.csv whose keywords appear in title or body; case-insensitive, multiple allowed)
   - matched_keywords (array of the exact keywords that triggered sector matches)
   - retrieved_at (ISO-8601 datetime of fetch)
   Only include items that have at least one sector match or contain one of: invest, trade, export, industry in the title or body.

3) Aggregation (compute basic statistics): Produce two CSV summaries under reports/:
   - reports/summary_by_region_sector.csv with columns: region,sector,article_count (count derived from data/extracted/items.jsonl)
   - reports/summary_by_source.csv with columns: source,domain,total_articles,avg_word_count

4) Reproducibility and constraints:
   - Implement the workflow as a script that can be executed from the command line (e.g., Python or shell). Place the main entry point at scripts/fetch_and_analyze.(py|sh) and run it to generate all outputs.
   - Deduplicate articles by URL. Ignore off-domain links. Skip non-200 responses gracefully.
   - The output paths and file formats named above are mandatory.

Deliverables to verify in the workspace after running:
- data/raw/<slug>/... (downloaded HTML files)
- data/fetch_manifest.csv (as specified)
- data/extracted/items.jsonl (one JSON per article with the required fields)
- reports/summary_by_region_sector.csv and reports/summary_by_source.csv

Use input/sources.csv and input/sectors.csv as the single sources of truth for domains and sector classification. Do not hardcode page URLs; discovery must start from each domain’s home page. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
